title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · C Contract Brief: Nulling Limitations
summary: An asynchronous job queue keeps aliases after cancellation and occasionally corrupts a newly allocated job at the same address. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem nulling limitations increment in generation-checked handle repair. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use nulling as a local state aid without claiming it repairs aliases, temporal races, or ownership ambiguity.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Nulling Limitations · Theory Nulling Limitations
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8326. Produce bounded C evidence to reject stale handles with a generation check; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
